commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3319aa4a11be96121139d65406eb9a2fbd74c3cd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Claude live `can_use_tool` allow responses and Codex native-hook `PermissionRequest` allow decisions to `updatedInput`, then updates focused tests to expect that shape.

PR surface: Source +3, Tests +3. Total +6 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main and `v2026.6.8` return `behavior: "allow"` on the Claude live `can_use_tool` allow path, and the linked issue supplies a concrete Claude Code 2.1.156 `ZodError`. I did not run a live Claude CLI session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code repair is narrow, but contributor-provided real behavior proof is still required and cannot be supplied by an automated repair worker.

target.title: fix(claude-cli): update PermissionResult to match Claude Code 2.1.x schema
target.body:
## Summary

Update the `can_use_tool` permission response from the deprecated
`{ behavior: "allow" }` to the Claude Code 2.1.x schema that expects
`{ updatedInput: <record> }`.

## Problem

Claude Code 2.1.156 tightened the `PermissionResult` schema. OpenClaw
returned `{ behavior: "allow" }` which validates against neither branch
of the new union, causing `ZodError` on every tool call.

Pinning Claude Code to 2.0.77 confirms the regression is on the
OpenClaw bridge side.

## Root Cause

Two locations returned the deprecated `{ behavior: "allow" }` shape:
- `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts:879`
- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts:396`

## Changes

- **`src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts`** — return `{ updatedInput: request.input }` instead of `{ behavior: "allow" }` for the allow path
- **`src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`** — return `{ updatedInput: {} }` instead of `{ behavior: "allow" }`; deny path unchanged

## Testing

- Verified against Claude Code 2.1.156: tool calls no longer throw ZodError
- Verified against Claude Code 2.0.77: backward-compatible (old CLI accepts updatedInput)
- Code review: both call sites return the new shape consistently

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Claude Code 2.1.x rejects OpenClaw's `can_use_tool` responses because they use the deprecated `{ behavior: "allow" }` shape instead of the new `{ updatedInput }` union branch
- Real environment tested: Code review at `src/agents/cli-runner/claude-live-session.ts:879` and `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts:396` — both sites were returning the old shape. The fix changes them to return `{ updatedInput }` which matches the Claude Code 2.1.x PermissionResult union
- Exact steps to verify: Run any tool call through the Claude CLI bridge with Claude Code >= 2.1.156. Before fix — ZodError on every tool call. After fix — tool calls succeed
- Observed result after fix: Both allow-path call sites now return `{ updatedInput }` matching the current Claude Code PermissionResult schema; deny paths are unchanged

## Related

Closes #95171
